revision.text:
[[ja:墓山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |name=Hakayama Kofun |image=File:Hakayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location={{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308|1=Habikino}} |Lat degrees = 34|Lat minutes = 33|Lat seconds = 27.12 |Lon degrees = 135|Lon minutes = 36|Lon seconds = 14.56 |omitted = JP-27 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (zenpokoenfun) |area=Total length: 225 m
Height: 20.7 m |burial= |built=Early 5th century |Buried individual= |burial goods= |Imperial tomb={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}-Historic Site1 satellite tomb of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Ega-no-Mofushikasa no Misasagi) |Historic Site=National Historic Site of Japan, included in the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" |notes=22nd largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Osaka |map_dot_label= |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun-west |coordinates={{Coord|34.557533|N|135.604044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''{{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}''' (墓山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, Historic Site1 as a UNESCO World Historic Site site. The tomb is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as a satellite tomb of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Ega-no-Mofushikasa no Misasagi). It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on February 22, 1975. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, southwest of the Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. Measuring approximately 225 meters in length, the rear circular mound is about 135 meters in diameter and 20.7 meters high, while the front section is 153 meters wide and 19.3 meters high. It is the fifth largest kofun in the cluster. The mound is constructed in three tiers, with fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures) confirmed. Both sides of the constricted area feature protrusions (tsukuridashi), and the kofun is surrounded by a 15-meter-wide moat and a 37-meter-wide embankment. The summit of the rear mound reportedly features the lid of a Sarcophagus (long box-shaped, made of Tatsuyama stone) engraved with a tortoise shell pattern. burial goods such as magatama (comma-shaped beads) made of talc and figurative haniwa are preserved by the Imperial Household Agency. Based on the haniwa and coffin characteristics, the kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century. == Satellite Tombs == [[File:墓山古墳と陪塚.jpg|thumb|{{National Aerial Photo}}]] The surrounding area features five square-shaped kofun, believed to be satellite tombs, including {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Kofun|qq=}}, Mukohakayama Kofun, Jogonjiyama Kofun, Nishihakayama Kofun, and Nishimazuka Kofun. Among these, the now-destroyed Nishihakayama Kofun, with a side length of 18 meters, yielded a large quantity of iron burial goods (over 200 swords and 2,000 agricultural tools) during excavation. No human remains were found, suggesting the kofun served as a tomb for grave goods.[http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,98,146.html Nishihakayama Kofun - Fujiidera City] Nishimazuka Kofun is associated with a legend in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} about a horse exchanged for another that transtypeed into a haniwa horse the next day. == Additional Intypeation == The southern moat of Hakayama Kofun was later filled in, with part of the outer embankment removed. The area is now used as a cemetery, which includes the grave of Momoi Shunzo. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|401|1798|Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,1041,96,139.html Fujiidera City: Hakayama Kofun and Nonaka Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} }} {{translated page|ja|墓山古墳|version=84488006|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:墓山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11429120}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
